bibliographic_data.title: DataStates-LLM: Lazy Asynchronous Checkpointing for Large Language Models
bibliographic_data.abstract: LLMs have seen rapid adoption in all domains and need to be trained on high-end HPC infrastructures, which can lead to unexpected events such as hardware failures or undesirable learning patterns. To address the challenge of frequent checkpointing for LLMs, this paper introduces a lazy asynchronous multi-level approach that reduces I/O overheads by copying tensor content in the background without significant interference during training. The results show up to 48× faster checkpointing and 2.2× faster end-to-end training runtime compared with state-of-the-art approaches.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper introduces a lazy asynchronous multi-level approach for checkpointing large language models (LLMs) that reduces I/O overheads and enables faster checkpointing and end-to-end training.
core_content.key_methodology: A lazy asynchronous multi-level approach is introduced, which leverages the immutability of model and optimizer state tensors to copy their content in the background with minimal interference during training. This method allows for frequent checkpointing at the granularity of individual iterations without significant impact on the training process.
outcomes: 2.2× faster end-to-end training runtime compared to state-of-the-art approaches
core_content.full_text_markdown: LLMs have seen rapid adoption in various domains and need to be trained on high-end HPC infrastructures, which can lead to unexpected events such as hardware failures or undesirable learning patterns. To address the challenge of frequent checkpointing for LLMs, this paper introduces a lazy asynchronous multi-level approach that reduces I/O overheads by copying tensor content in the background with minimal interference during training. The results show up to 48× faster checkpointing and 2.2× faster end-to-end training runtime compared with state-of-the-art approaches.
